definitions.technical: The alternative approach to reserve diversification that imposes mandatory numerical limits on concentration, including a 40% cap on reserves held at any single institution. The comment letter recommends this approach.
definitions.lay: A proposed approach that sets firm numerical limits on how stablecoin reserves must be spread out. For example, no more than 40% of reserves can be held at any one bank. The comment letter supports this approach because the numbers create clear, auditable rules.
definitions.parenthetical: (a mandatory quantitative reserve diversification standard with a 40% single-institution concentration cap)
definitions.legal_significance: The comment letter recommends Option B because its quantitative thresholds create auditable benchmarks compatible with the monthly attestation cycle. The 40% concentration cap directly addresses the SVB scenario and provides a clear enforcement standard.
